This movie was awesome..I absolutely loved it. Never has an Invisible Man movie been so scary. It was almost like a possession or haunting film, especially with the orchestrated soundtrack. This is a must see film. We just got out of the movies earlier!!  My friend Glenn went with me and my wife to the show. This brand new The Invisible Man that came out yesterday was the best movie I've seen in a while! Absolutely awesome film. They turned the idea of The Invisible Man into a very violent and scary story you would not expect. It also has a very wicked orchestrated soundtrack reminiscent of an older horror film. It sends chills down your spine. The storyline is how the wife of a psychopathical scientist who developes high tech experimental gadgetry, stalks her in invisible form, after convincing everyone of his death,  and terrorizes her, after she flees their home in the middle of the night to escape him, since he is terrorizing in her everyday life as well. She soon realizes he is not dead, but invisible and hunting her. There are scenes after he finds her where she is being held up off the ground and beaten by this invisible man, kind of like in the old thriller The Entity or a demonic possession thriller. He kills people violently and blames it on her. She ends up being committed to an insane asylum since everyone thinks she's imagining it. But eventually she finds a way to fight back. This movie was awesome!!! I give it 10 out 10 skulls..💀💀💀💀💀💀💀💀💀💀

I really do not know what to make of this trailer. On the one hand, being stalked is a very real thing, that is truly horrifying and difficult to deal with, so making the stalker a literal movie monster is really upping the horror of that situation, and it seems they are going for a Rosemary's Baby type of horror, where no one believes the protagonist as well, which is a very effective type of horror. On the other hand, it's rather odd to see a Universal movie monster with the (apparently) singular goal of just ruining one persons life. If the women being stalked dies or disappears, does the Invisible Man have to get a new girlfriend to continue obsessing over, or is he just done? It also seems like a pretty solvable problem, unless for some reason the woman being stalked here has no friends or credibility at all, or the Invisible Man has other super-powers. It will be interesting to see if the writer is really creative, or just lazy.
